page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-32 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance configuration does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the c4a-standard-32 is designed for general-purpose workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention. This profile is suitable for multi-threaded applications, web serving, application servers, and moderate database workloads that require balanced compute and memory resources without the need for local high-speed storage or hardware acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-32 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the c4a server family. It provides 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. This instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage solutions for data persistence. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for general-purpose workloads, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures stable performance under sustained compute demands. The c4a-standard-32 is suited for web serving, application hosting, containerized microservices, and moderate database deployments requiring a balanced distribution of processing power and memory capacity without specialized hardware acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-32 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, designed for balanced general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-32 is an x86_64 instance with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual instance featuring thirty-two dedicated vCPUs and one hundred twenty-eight gigabytes of memory for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 128.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included
Suited for web serving, application hosting, and moderate databases
